demandLinks.relevance: fresh-state observation, explicit terminal states, evidence receipts, and honest stop or no-delta outcomes
one-shot-versus-loop qualification, bounded repetition, fallback, and context or control cost
record, save, catalog, publication, ownership, license, and external-write boundaries

strengths: zero additional Skill context and no new source, catalog, persistence, or publication surface
best fit when feedback cannot materially change the next action or the task is exploratory, creative, low-risk, or already has an obvious terminal check
limits: general feedback-loop qualification, terminal-state receipts, and debrief discipline may be applied inconsistently
repeatable cross-task loop design is not packaged as a discoverable reusable capability

strengths: the adaptive Harness already preserves open, assist, guarded, native, no-Skill, no-Hook, and safe-fallback outcomes

diagnose provides a strong domain-specific reproducible debugging loop, while current intake, review, handoff, shipping, and closeout capabilities cover authority, evidence, verification, continuation, and release boundaries
limits: the current chain does not expose one compact general-purpose contract for deciding whether an arbitrary task should loop, running a bounded observe-choose-act-verify-record cycle, and debriefing the loop itself
the repository Harness loop governs curation metabolism rather than directly supplying a user-task feedback-loop Skill

strengths: explicitly rejects looping when feedback cannot change the next action
combines fresh-state observation, bounded action, reproducible verification, recording, terminal states, no-op handling, independent verification, and post-run debrief in one portable body
requires at least two recurrences before claiming a reusable pattern and treats publication as a separate approved external action
limits: the 15.5 KB body duplicates current authority, verification, and closeout controls and may add friction to simple or divergent work
find and publish depend on a live external catalog, while save can create or change project LOOPS.md state; these are separate data and write boundaries
full-body superiority, cross-Agent activation behavior, context cost, false-positive loop selection, and sustained net benefit are unverified

marginalValueHypothesis.distinctiveIncrement: A general task-level loop qualification and bounded execution receipt: use one-shot when feedback cannot change the next action; otherwise observe fresh state, choose one bounded action, verify an observable gate, record evidence, and stop or repeat explicitly.
notDistinctive: debug-specific reproduction loops already covered by diagnose
repository curation lifecycle feedback already covered by the Harness
marginalValueHypothesis.sourcePreservingRule: Do not rewrite or partially vendor the upstream body merely to remove overlap. Either validate and admit an exact pinned Skill body under a bounded route, keep it reference-only, or reject it. A new repository-authored capability remains ineligible unless a reproducible residual gap survives this comparison.

scenarioMatrix.shape: a reversible local task where each test result can change the next action
scenarioMatrix.expectedPreferredRoute: compare native/current-chain against the exact Loopy body
observe: time to a valid feedback gate

scenarioMatrix.shape: a bounded analysis where feedback cannot change the next action
observe: whether Loopy exits cleanly without ceremony

scenarioMatrix.shape: a task whose repeat cycle reaches publish, production, financial, privacy, or external-message boundaries
scenarioMatrix.expectedPreferredRoute: guarded current chain with explicit authority; Loopy may assist but cannot grant authority

scenarioMatrix.shape: a hard bug requiring a fast deterministic reproduction loop
scenarioMatrix.expectedPreferredRoute: diagnose unless Loopy demonstrates a material task-specific increment

decision.nextGate: fixture-only paired behavior comparison across the four scenario shapes; no live Agent, Hook, catalog, publication, or consumer-configuration mutation

recheckTriggers: the pinned Loopy body, license, catalog contract, publication contract, or maintainer state changes
paired scenarios show a stable material benefit or material friction against native and current-chain alternatives
a concrete task proposes installation, save, catalog access, publication, admission, adaptation, or self-authoring
